subject: Physics
title: Einstein and the Photon
description: A fast breakdown of Einstein and the Photon for builders.

hook.text: Einstein took Planck's math trick and made it real—light actually comes in particles called photons.

buildup.text: When light hits metal, electrons pop out. Brighter light ejects more electrons but not faster ones.

discovery.text: Einstein explained this: each photon carries a fixed energy. Higher frequency means more energy per photon.

twist.text: This won Einstein his only Nobel Prize—not relativity. Photons were more convincing to the committee.

climax.text: The photon concept proved light is both a wave and a particle, launching quantum physics for real.

punchline.text: Light comes in packets. Einstein proved it. Physics split open.

quiz.question: What did Einstein explain with the photon concept?
